text.title: Branded Types and Const Assertions
text.summary: Branded types for preventing primitive mix-ups, const assertions for literal narrowing, and template literal string contracts.

rule.statement: Brand primitive ID types to prevent accidental swap between semantically distinct IDs of the same underlying type.
rule.rationale: UserId and OrderId are both strings — without branding, passing one where the other is expected compiles silently and fails at the data layer.

rule.statement: Use "as const" on configuration objects and action-type maps to narrow to literal types and derive union types from values.
rule.rationale: Without as const, string properties widen to string; with it they narrow to their literal values, enabling exhaustive discriminated unions.

rule.statement: Use template literal types to enforce URL prefix and HTTP method contracts at the type level.
rule.rationale: Functions accepting arbitrary strings admit typos; template literal types constrain the shape without runtime checks.
